sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see there are 3 red buttons and 3 green buttons. The red buttons are on the left side and the green buttons are on the right side. I've been clicking the top red button at (17,20) which rotates the ring clockwise.
There are also middle and bottom red/green buttons. Maybe these control different aspects of the puzzle.
Let me think about this differently. There are 3 pairs of red/green buttons:
1. Top: (17,20) red, (17,39) green
2. Middle: (26,14) red, (26,48) green
3. Bottom: (35,14) red, (35,48) green
Maybe each pair controls a different ring or a different part of the puzzle. But I only see one ring of 26 blocks.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the 3 pairs of buttons control 3 different rings. Let me look at the board more carefully.
Looking at the current frame, I see:
- A ring of 26 blocks around the center
- Fixed blocks at rows 26 and 35
- The ring blocks at (26,23), (26,35), (35,23), (35,35) are between the fixed blocks
Maybe there are 3 separate rings or rows that can be rotated independently. Let me check if clicking the middle or bottom buttons rotates different parts of the ring.
Actually, looking at the ring positions I defined earlier, the ring goes around the entire perimeter. The top, middle, and bottom buttons might all control the same ring, or they might control different segments.
Let me try clicking the middle green button to see if it rotates the ring counter-clockwise.
